title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, and Invalidation · Type Forensics
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible cache identity and freshness contract with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cache identity and freshness contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Cache Key Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5750 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to align typed cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, and release freshness; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.

Inspect the Cache Key Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Cache Key Identity.
